content.claim: School choice and decentralization in Finland (1990s onward) are associated with rising school segregation in metropolitan areas, but the causal weighting between choice-driven enrollment patterns versus residential sorting remains contested among Finnish scholars.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Swedish evidence (choice > residential sorting effect) cited but Finnish sources split attribution; mechanism clarity limited
Segregation measured by school-level outcomes or demographic composition; unclear whether segregation represents unequal access or unequal outcomes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
